channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'So we've got a proper four wall bedroom situation': I asked Alexa+ to help redecorate my apartment, and she has a great eye for design
item.description: It turns out Amazon's AI-powered assistant has a much better eye for design than me — and it really loves sage green.

item.title: “Tokenmaxxing” is making developers less productive than they think
item.description: There's a lot more code—but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

item.title: What is Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen about?
item.description: Interested in checking out Netflix's "Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen"? Read on for what to expect.

item.title: 'I’ve waited seven years!': Tesla owners in Europe demand $7,500 refund as Full Self-Driving is locked out for older vehicles
item.description: Anger rises in Europe as Tesla's Full Self-Driving system isn't available on older vehicles, despite years of promises

item.title: Betting on the news raises ethical questions for journalists
item.description: Prediction market exchanges have created an environment where just about any piece of information is potentially monetizable: How well will BTS's new song perform this week? How hot will Los Angeles get? Will Donald Trump be impeached? Users can wager on all of that and, on some platforms, more gruesome and violent outcomes in the […]

item.title: Steph Curry is testing Google and Fitbits screenless Whoop competitor: Everything we know
item.description: Steph Curry has been wearing a new screenless wearable like the Whoop fitness tracker. Here's what we know about the Fitbit device.

item.title: Blatantly fake news about college sports spreads like wildfire in the absence of player payday details
item.description: NIH deals are closely guarded, but X accounts are happy to fill the information void with falsehoods, amassing large followings in the process. About an hour after the men’s college basketball season ended in Indianapolis with a Michigan Wolverines’ championship on April 6, the team’s coaching staff was already working hard at trying to win the next one....

item.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
item.description: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vulnerabilities in real-life attacks, according to a cybersecurity firm.

item.title: ‘We don’t want to be left behind’: Reese Witherspoon says using AI is feminist and women need to catch up
item.description: Witherspoon warned women that their careers are likely to taken by AI if they don’t start learning about the tech. That’s prompted a social media outcry. Reese Witherspoon has established herself as a businesswoman committed to feminism. Her media brand Hello Sunshine’s mission is to “put women at the center of every story,” and her much-memed quote that “women’s stories matter—they just matter!” is nearly as recognizable as her roles in Legally Blonde and Big Little Lies....

item.title: This charming gadget writes bad AI poetry
item.description: I've never been as charmed and frustrated by one gadget as I have with the Poetry Camera. It's a delightful object. White and cherry red with a color-matched woven strap, it looks playful and adorably lo-fi. If I saw it on a store shelf, I'd absolutely pick it up. But aside from obviously appealing, I'm […]

item.title: Anthropic says Claude Opus 4.7 has a 92% honesty rate, less sycophancy
item.description: Anthropic says its new AI model Claude Opus 4.7 is less likely to hallucinate and engage in sycophancy than most other models.

item.title: Zoom teams up with World to verify humans in meetings
item.description: Zoom will show a badge on verified participants' tile.

item.title: Sam Altman's 'human verification' company thinks its eye-scanning orbs could solve ticket scalping
item.description: Sam Altman's cryptocurrency turned identity verification startup Tools for Humanity is offering a new set of perks to people who scan their eyes at one of the company's orbs. Among them, is a new tool called Concert Kit that could help bands and artists fight back against ticket scalping bots. The new feature relies on the revamped World ID, the orb-based verification system that scans users eyeballs and faces to create a "proof of human" signature that lives on users' mobile devices. "It's basically like a little human passport for the internet that lets you prove on apps and websites that yo...

item.title: Gazing Into Sam Altman’s Orb Now Proves You’re Human on Tinder
item.description: Honestly, what’s hotter than a real person?

item.title: Jim Farley on why Ford is doubling down on affordable EVs
item.description: With oil prices surging and Chinese automakers gaining ground worldwide, Farley says Ford’s early EV missteps helped clarify what customers actually want now. With the Strait of Hormuz in crisis and gas prices surging, few executives are feeling the pressure more acutely than Ford Motor Company CEO Jim Farley. He gives a candid account of what the turmoil means for the auto industry, and for an iconic American brand navigating one of the most turbulent moments in its history. Plus, Farley gets frank about the China threat reshaping the global auto business, and his frustration with Ford’s own ...

item.title: MG4 à batterie semi-solide : avancée majeure ou mirage marketing ? Les ingénieurs s’expliquent
item.description: Les batteries solides sont attendues comme le graal des batteries pour voitures électriques. Cependant, leur mise en production demande beaucoup de temps avant d’aboutir à un produit fiable et à un prix raisonnable. MG a alors trouvé un compromis : la batterie semi-solide, qui arrive dès cette année en Europe sur la MG4. On a pu discuter avec des ingénieurs chinois responsables du programme. ...
